sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Jupiter Neurosciences Expands Nugevia™ Addressable Market with Focus on GLP-1 Users
feed.summary: Jupiter Neurosciences is expanding the market for its Nugevia™ longevity product line to support individuals using GLP-1-based weight loss therapies. The company believes Nugevia™ can address unmet needs related to mitochondrial energy production, lean tissue preservation, and neuro-gut inflammatory balance, which can be stressed during GLP-1-induced weight loss. This strategy combines their clinical-stage pharmaceutical pipeline with a direct-to-consumer longevity business, both powered by their proprietary JOTROL™ resveratrol formulation.

feed.title: Wells Fargo’s FiNet Recruits Financial Advisors From Ameriprise, Raymond James, UBS
feed.summary: Wells Fargo's independent wealth management channel, FiNet, has successfully recruited financial advisors from Ameriprise, Raymond James, and UBS. These new recruits previously oversaw a combined total of over $1.3 billion in assets at their former firms. This marks a series of recruiting successes for Wells Fargo as it aims to strengthen its FiNet division.

feed.title: Air Products and Chemicals, Inc. (NYSE:APD) Raises Dividend to $1.81 Per Share
feed.summary: Air Products and Chemicals, Inc. (NYSE:APD) has announced a quarterly dividend increase to $1.81 per share, up 1.1% from its previous dividend. The ex-dividend date is April 1st, with the payment scheduled for May 11th. The company has a strong dividend history, having raised its dividend for 43 consecutive years, and analysts expect its earnings to continue covering future dividend payments.

feed.title: Merck & Co., Inc. Plans Quarterly Dividend of $0.85 (NYSE:MRK)
feed.summary: Merck & Co., Inc. (NYSE:MRK) announced a quarterly dividend of $0.85 per share, payable on April 7th to shareholders of record by March 16th. This translates to an annual dividend yield of 3.2%, with the company having consistently raised its dividend for the past 14 years. Analysts project strong earnings for Merck & Co., Inc. next year, indicating continued ability to cover its dividend payments.

feed.title: HP Inc. to Issue Quarterly Dividend of $0.30 (NYSE:HPQ)
feed.summary: HP Inc. (NYSE:HPQ) has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.30 per share, payable on April 1st to shareholders of record on March 11th. The company maintains a 15-year history of annual dividend increases and a low payout ratio, indicating good coverage by earnings. HP's stock recently traded up slightly, with a market capitalization of $17.85 billion.

feed.title: General Mills, Inc. Announces Quarterly Dividend of $0.61 (NYSE:GIS)
feed.summary: General Mills, Inc. (NYSE:GIS) has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.61 per share, payable on May 1st to shareholders of record on April 10th. The company has consistently increased its dividend for the past five years and maintains a sustainable payout ratio. Analysts anticipate General Mills will continue to cover its dividend with future earnings, despite recent stock trading showing minimal change.

feed.title: CF Industries Holdings, Inc. (NYSE:CF) Declares Quarterly Dividend of $0.50
feed.summary: CF Industries Holdings, Inc. (NYSE:CF) announced a quarterly dividend of $0.50 per share, payable on February 27th to shareholders of record by February 13th. The company has consistently increased its dividend and maintains a conservative payout ratio, with analysts expecting continued coverage by future earnings. Despite trading up 1.1% on the announcement, analysts generally maintain a "Hold" rating on the stock with an average target price of $89.71.

feed.title: Farmers National Banc Corp. to Issue Quarterly Dividend of $0.17 (NASDAQ:FMNB)
feed.summary: Farmers National Banc Corp. (NASDAQ:FMNB) declared a quarterly dividend of $0.17 per share, payable on February 27th to shareholders of record by February 13th, representing an annualized yield of 5.4%. The company has a dividend payout ratio of 43.0% and analysts expect it to cover future dividends with a projected payout ratio of 43.9%. Despite recent market trading down and missing earnings estimates, analysts maintain a "Hold" rating with an average price target of $14.50.

feed.title: AmEx Set to Report Q4 Earnings: Key Metrics Investors Should Track
feed.summary: American Express Company (AXP) is slated to release its fourth-quarter 2025 earnings on January 30, 2026. Analysts predict a 16.8% year-over-year increase in earnings per share to $3.55 and a 9.6% rise in revenues to $18.82 billion, driven by resilient consumer spending and increased network volumes. Factors like growing interest income and cards-in-force are expected to contribute positively, though higher customer engagement and operating costs could partially offset these gains, leading to an uncertain earnings beat according to Zacks' model.

feed.title: Keysight Technologies (NYSE:KEYS) Sets New 12-Month High - Here's What Happened
feed.summary: Keysight Technologies (NYSE:KEYS) reached a new 52-week high of $221.22 on Wednesday, closing at $218.7880. This surge follows recent analyst target increases and positive Q4 (2025) earnings, where the company surpassed consensus estimates. The company also announced a $1.50 billion stock buyback program, signaling confidence from its board.

feed.title: SLJY: 18% Target Income Silver Covered Call ETF To Buy (NYSEARCA:SLJY)
feed.summary: SLJY is a relatively new silver miner covered call ETF that uses deep out-of-the-money options to provide a target income of 18%. The ETF focuses on junior silver mining companies, which have a strong correlation with physical silver but have recently diverged from its surge. The author views SLJY as a mean-reversion play and an investable option for income investors, explaining its structure and potential integration into investor portfolios.

feed.title: Altria (MO) Set to Report Q4 Earnings Amid Competition with Philip Morris
feed.summary: Altria Group (MO) is poised to release its Q4 earnings with an anticipated 2.3% EPS increase despite a projected 1.4% revenue decline, highlighting its ongoing reliance on traditional cigarette sales. The company faces stiff competition from Philip Morris (PM), which excels in smoke-free product innovation like ZYN oral pouches. Despite strong profitability metrics, Altria's financial health presents challenges with its high dividend payout ratio and valuation metrics nearing historical highs.

feed.title: Palo Alto highlights quantum-safe security initiatives
feed.summary: Palo Alto Networks recently held a Quantum-Safe Summit to address the increasing threats quantum computing poses to current encryption. Experts highlighted the urgent need for a shift to post-quantum cryptography, with governments like the US mandating changes by 2030, to counter "harvest now, decrypt later" threats. Wedbush analysts commend Palo Alto Networks for proactive initiatives, including a partnership with IBM, positioning the company to benefit from future demand for quantum-safe cybersecurity.

feed.title: Lam Research and Generac have been highlighted as Zacks Bull and Bear of the Day
feed.summary: Zacks Equity Research has named Lam Research (LRCX) as its Bull of the Day due to its strong position in wafer fabrication equipment, particularly for AI and HPC applications, and its critical role in advanced transistor designs, with analysts raising price targets and EPS estimates. Conversely, Generac Holdings (GNRC) is designated as the Bear of the Day due to flat revenue projections for 2025, declining profits, and missed third-quarter EPS estimates, influenced by weaker-than-expected seasonal demand for generators and increasing competition. The article also provides analysis on Micron Technology, Inc. (MU) and NVIDIA Corp. (NVDA), highlighting Micron's growth potential from surging HBM demand.

feed.title: Merck Announces Second-Quarter 2026 Dividend
feed.summary: Merck (NYSE: MRK) has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.85 per share for the second quarter of 2026. This dividend will be paid on April 7, 2026, to shareholders of record as of March 16, 2026. The announcement highlights Merck's commitment to delivering innovative health solutions and operating responsibly.

feed.title: W.W. Grainger Keeps Quarterly Dividend at $2.26 per Share, Payable March 1 to Holders of Record Feb. 9
feed.summary: W.W. Grainger (GWW) announced that its board of directors has maintained the quarterly dividend at $2.26 per share. This dividend is payable on March 1 to shareholders of record as of February 9. The announcement was made on January 28, 2026.

feed.title: Microchip Technology (MCHP) Stock Trades Up, Here Is Why
feed.summary: Microchip Technology's stock jumped 6.8% after Bank of America upgraded it to Buy from Neutral, citing signs of recovery in the analog chip market and increased demand from data centers, aerospace, and autos. The bank also raised its price target to $95 and increased its 2026 and 2027 earnings estimates. MCHP has seen significant volatility but the market views this news positively, contributing to its year-to-date gain of 24.8% and a new 52-week high.

feed.title: Stride, Inc. (NYSE:LRN) Q2 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
feed.summary: Stride, Inc. reported its Q2 2026 earnings, discussing strong financial performance despite missing EPS expectations. The company highlighted significant growth in gross margins, EBITDA, and operational PAT, driven by a focus on profitability over revenue growth and strategic discipline in its US and international markets. Management addressed future growth strategies, debt reduction, and R&D investments, particularly in nasal spray products, emphasizing a long-term sustainable business approach.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs Raises Price Target on Expedia Group (EXPE) to $325
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs analyst Eric Sheridan has reiterated his 'Buy' rating for Expedia Group (EXPE) and increased the price target from $295 to $325, marking a 10.17% raise. This adjustment follows several other analysts who have recently updated their price targets for the online travel agency. Despite the optimistic analyst forecasts, GuruFocus estimates a potential downside of 30.42% for EXPE based on its GF Value.

feed.title: Deere calls back nearly 100 laid-off Iowa employees after Trump visit
feed.summary: John Deere is recalling nearly 100 laid-off employees to its Davenport and Dubuque plants in Iowa, just one day after President Trump praised the company during a visit. The callbacks are attributed to increased production demand for its construction and forestry equipment. This move comes after significant layoffs in 2024 and 2025, reflecting a challenging period for the manufacturing sector in Iowa and for Deere due to a downturn in agriculture and issues like tariffs.

feed.title: New KAYAK for Business Events tool takes on messy corporate travel
feed.summary: KAYAK for Business has launched a new "Events" tool designed to streamline corporate travel planning for events. This feature allows planners to manage travel for up to 10,000 attendees, set policies, and track bookings, aiming to reduce complexity and ensure compliance. The tool integrates with existing event management systems and provides real-time dashboards for improved visibility.

feed.title: Grainger to pay $2.26 per share to investors on March 1
feed.summary: W.W. Grainger, Inc. (NYSE: GWW) announced a quarterly cash dividend of $2.26 per share, payable on March 1, 2026, to shareholders of record on February 9, 2026. This dividend aligns with the company's commitment to a disciplined capital-allocation strategy and delivering long-term shareholder value. Grainger, a leading broad line distributor with 2024 revenues of $17.2 billion, serves over 4.6 million customers in North America and Japan.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Believes "Broadcom Represents One of the Cheaper Ways to Play Artificial Intelligence"
feed.summary: Jim Cramer recommends buying Broadcom (NASDAQ: AVGO) stock, viewing it as an undervalued way to invest in artificial intelligence, despite recent market declines. He expresses strong confidence in CEO Hock Tan to continue delivering strong results, highlighting Broadcom's solid performance in both software and hardware. Cramer suggests that the recent dip in the stock presents a good buying opportunity.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Adjusts Automatic Data Processing PT to $262 From $272
feed.summary: Wells Fargo has revised its price target for Automatic Data Processing (ADP) shares, lowering it to $262 from the previous $272. This adjustment comes shortly after ADP announced its fiscal Q2 earnings, which saw increased adjusted earnings and revenue, leading to an updated fiscal 2026 outlook. The company also recently highlighted its advancements in AI technology for payroll and HR tasks.

feed.title: Scotiabank, Citi, and Raymond James Raise Newmont (NEM) Price Targets
feed.summary: Scotiabank, Citi, and Raymond James have all raised their price targets for Newmont Corporation (NEM). Scotiabank increased its target due to updated gold and silver price forecasts, citing economic uncertainty and central bank buying. Citi maintained its Buy rating while acknowledging a potential moderation in gold prices, and Raymond James highlighted Newmont's strong global portfolio and flexible balance sheet.

feed.title: Behind the Scenes of Oklo's Latest Options Trends
feed.summary: Whales have shown a significantly bullish stance on Oklo (NYSE: OKLO), initiating 76 options trades, with 40% bullish and 32% bearish. The options activity suggests a target price range of $40.0 to $200.0 for Oklo over the last three months, with a current stock price of $91.47 and a recent 7.27% increase. Analyst ratings are mixed but generally positive, with an average target price of $132.5.

feed.title: Corning Q4 Earnings Call Highlights
feed.summary: Corning reported strong Q4 and full-year 2025 results, with Q4 sales up 14% to $4.41 billion and core EPS up 26% to $0.72. The company also raised its Springboard ambitions, targeting $11 billion in incremental sales by 2028, and announced a multi-year agreement worth up to $6 billion with Meta. Optical Communications was identified as the primary growth driver, with significant investments planned for capacity expansion.

feed.title: Blackstone Q4 earnings preview: What to expect (BX:NYSE)
feed.summary: Blackstone (BX) is set to release its Q4 earnings on January 29th, with analysts expecting an EPS of $1.54 and revenue of $3.69 billion. Despite potential risks from a Trump executive order on single-family home purchases, the company's assets under management are projected to reach $1.27 trillion. Wall Street and Seeking Alpha authors rate BX as a Buy, while SA's Quant rating is a Hold.

feed.title: Boeing reports $565M loss on KC-46 as firm looks forward to repricing
feed.summary: Boeing reported a $565 million loss on its KC-46 Pegasus refueling tanker program in the fourth quarter of 2025, adding to billions in previous losses. Despite the setback, the company sees encouraging operational performance and aims to reprice future KC-46 contracts to ensure profitability, especially as the Air Force plans to purchase more units on a sole-source basis. Boeing attributes the loss mainly to higher supply chain and production support costs, and is investing in quality improvements at its Everett facility.

feed.title: Apple’s Creator Studio Isn’t an Adobe Killer. It’s Something Else Entirely
feed.summary: Apple has launched Creator Studio, a subscription service bundling its creative and productivity apps for $12.99 a month, significantly less than Adobe's Creative Cloud. While seemingly positioned to undercut Adobe, the author argues that Apple is targeting "creators" rather than established professionals, recognizing different needs and a market underserved by existing professional tools. The new bundle, including Final Cut Pro and Logic Pro, aims to expand Apple's reach to a broader creative audience.

feed.title: U.S. AES Corp. subsidiary cancels mega project in Chilean desert
feed.summary: AES Andes, a subsidiary of U.S.-based AES Corp., has canceled its controversial $10 billion INNA project in Chile, which aimed to produce green hydrogen and ammonia. The project faced significant opposition from Chile's astronomical community due to concerns about light pollution impacting the nearby Paranal Observatory. AES Andes stated it will now prioritize other renewable energy and storage projects in line with its parent company's strategy.

feed.title: Southern Copper Sees Lower Output as Silver Rally Grabs Focus
feed.summary: Southern Copper Corporation anticipates a decline in copper output for the next two years due to falling ore grades at its main Peruvian mines. Despite this, the company is shifting its focus towards increasing silver production, which is typically a byproduct, spurred by a rally in silver prices. The CFO projects copper output to be around 911,400 tons this year and slightly above 900,000 tons in 2027, a decrease from the 954,270 tons produced last year.

feed.title: Organon Completes Divestiture of JADA® System to Laborie
feed.summary: Organon (NYSE: OGN) announced the successful closing of the sale of its JADA® System to Laborie Medical Technologies Corp. The JADA® System is designed for the control and treatment of abnormal postpartum uterine bleeding or hemorrhage. Organon, a global healthcare company, focuses on women's health and general medicines, aiming to deliver impactful solutions for a healthier everyday.

feed.title: Analysts Cut Bank of America (BAC) Price Targets While Staying Bullish
feed.summary: Several analysts, including TD Cowen, Keefe, Bruyette & Woods, and Truist Securities, recently reduced their price targets for Bank of America (BAC) but maintained bullish ratings. These adjustments followed BAC's Q4 2025 earnings beat, which was offset by concerns over the company's near-term operating leverage guidance and slower share buyback expectations. Despite the target cuts, analysts believe Bank of America's revenue growth outlook largely remains intact.

feed.title: Amazon's encroachment in grocery not necessarily a bad thing for Instacart -- Morgan Stanley (CART:NASDAQ)
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley suggests that Amazon's expansion into grocery delivery, while having caused a 25% stock correction for Instacart, might not be entirely negative for the company. Instacart has competitive advantages like a loyal subscriber base, wider grocery selection, and strong retailer partnerships that differentiate it from Amazon. Analysts believe the recent sell-off may present a buying opportunity for Instacart, which is expected to show robust Q4 growth.

feed.title: Stride Earnings Strengthen Case For Career Learning Growth And Undervalued Shares
feed.summary: Stride (NYSE:LRN) recently announced strong earnings and significant enrollment growth in its career learning programs, leading to improved gross margins. The company is focusing on long-term stability by managing student intake and divesting non-core assets to concentrate on education and career learning. Despite a volatile share price, the stock is considered undervalued by analysts and Simply Wall St's valuation.

feed.title: Sysco Corp Is Feeding America – But Is SYY Stock Actually a Must-Cop Right Now?
feed.summary: Sysco Corp (SYY) is a major foodservice distributor that quietly underpins the restaurant and hospitality industry. The article explores whether SYY stock is a worthwhile investment, highlighting its stability and dividend potential for long-term investors, contrasting it with high-growth, high-volatility meme stocks. It concludes that SYY is suitable for those building a diversified, long-term portfolio based on real-world infrastructure.

feed.title: 10 Industrials Stocks Whale Activity In Today's Session
feed.summary: This article identifies significant "whale activity" in the Industrials sector, specifically focusing on options trading for ten different stocks. It details bullish and bearish call and put option trades for companies like Uber, Caterpillar, Lockheed Martin, and Axon Enterprise, providing information on strike prices, expiration dates, and contract volumes. The report defines key options terminology for traders and highlights how unusual trading volumes can indicate potential market shifts.

feed.title: 10 Information Technology Stocks Whale Activity In Today's Session
feed.summary: This article highlights significant "whale activity" in the options market for ten Information Technology stocks, indicating large-scale option trades that could signal future price movements. It details specific bullish, bearish, and neutral option sweeps and trades for companies like NVIDIA, Intel, Microsoft, and Advanced Micro Devices, providing contract details, strike prices, and expiration dates for each transaction. The piece aims to help traders identify potential opportunities by tracking these substantial options market transactions.

feed.title: Roper Technologies (ROP) Margin Compression Challenges Bullish Earnings Growth Narrative
feed.summary: Roper Technologies (ROP) has concluded FY 2025 with strong fourth-quarter revenue and basic EPS, yet its trailing 12-month net profit margin has decreased from 22% to 19.4%. This margin compression introduces a cautious note for investors, despite forecasts for considerable earnings growth (12.2%) outpacing revenue growth (9.3%). The article highlights that while valuation metrics suggest the stock is not aggressively priced, with a P/E of 25.9x and significant upside to DCF fair value, the company's high debt level warrants careful consideration.

feed.title: PREVIEW: Sherwin-Williams EPS, revenue expected to tick higher
feed.summary: Sherwin-Williams is projected to see an increase in its earnings per share (EPS) and revenue, according to analyst expectations. This preview suggests a positive outlook for the company's financial performance. The report is brief but highlights the anticipation of higher figures for the paint and coating manufacturer.

feed.title: Synchrony Financial (NYSE:SYF) Price Target Cut to $85.00 by Analysts at Royal Bank Of Canada
feed.summary: Royal Bank of Canada has reduced its price target for Synchrony Financial (NYSE:SYF) from $91.00 to $85.00, while maintaining a "sector perform" rating. This adjustment follows Synchrony's strong Q4 results, which beat analyst expectations, and the company's announcement of a $1.0 billion share buyback program and a $0.30 quarterly dividend. Analyst sentiment for Synchrony Financial is currently a "Moderate Buy" despite some concerns regarding restructuring charges, increasing costs, regulatory risks, and recent insider selling.

feed.title: BNY Mellon (BK) Price Targets Raised by KBW and RBC Capital
feed.summary: Keefe, Bruyette & Woods (KBW) raised its price target on BNY Mellon (BK) from $132 to $143, maintaining an Outperform rating, citing strong performance and new profitability targets. RBC Capital also increased its price target from $124 to $130, keeping a Sector Perform rating, recognizing the company's focus on execution. BNY Mellon is a leading financial services company, and both firms highlighted its positive outlook regarding financial metrics and operational efficiency.

feed.title: Roper Technologies (NASDAQ:ROP) Price Target Cut to $398.00 by Analysts at Royal Bank Of Canada
feed.summary: Royal Bank of Canada has lowered its price target for Roper Technologies (NASDAQ:ROP) to $398.00 from $539.00, assigning a "sector perform" rating. This adjustment follows mixed Q4 results where adjusted EPS beat expectations but revenue slightly missed, and management issued Q1 and FY2026 guidance below analyst estimates. Other analysts have also reduced price targets and ratings, contributing to a consensus "Hold" rating for the stock, which recently hit a 52-week low.

feed.title: UBS Lifts Altria (MO) Price Target, Keeps Buy Rating
feed.summary: UBS has increased its price target for Altria Group, Inc. (MO) from $63 to $67, while maintaining a Buy rating on the stock. The firm believes challenges like combustible volume and nicotine pouch sales are manageable and expects Altria to return to revenue growth and accelerate EPS in 2026 and 2027. Additionally, Altria's CEO, Billy Gifford, is set to retire in May 2026, with Salvatore Mancuso named as his successor.

feed.title: Scotiabank, Citi, and Raymond James Raise Newmont (NEM) Price Targets
feed.summary: Scotiabank, Citi, and Raymond James have all recently increased their price targets for Newmont Corporation (NEM). Scotiabank's increase to $152 and maintained Outperform rating are due to updated gold and precious minerals forecasts amidst economic and geopolitical uncertainty. Citi raised its target to $118 with a Buy rating, while Raymond James set its target at $111 with an Outperform rating, citing Newmont's global gold portfolio with lower jurisdictional risk and flexible balance sheet.

feed.title: Elevance making difficult decisions amid healthcare minefield
feed.summary: Elevance Health reported a significant profit increase in Q4 2025 despite rising costs but faces challenges in Medicaid, Medicare, and ACA markets. The company plans a year of "execution and repositioning" to strengthen performance and align cost structures. Elevance is adjusting pricing, refining products, and selectively investing to return to profit targets amidst policy-driven changes and increased medical cost trends.

feed.title: Here’s Why Wall Street is Bullish on Flex Ltd. (FLEX)
feed.summary: Wall Street holds a strong bullish outlook on Flex Ltd. (FLEX), with all 12 analysts covering the stock issuing Buy ratings. This optimism is partly due to a recent upgrade from Raymond James, citing Flex's robust position in cloud and AI infrastructure, especially its differentiated power solutions for hyperscale data centers. Analysts anticipate strong near-term growth, particularly in the company's high-margin power business, which is expected to boost overall profitability.

feed.title: The Honest Company (HNST) Declined Amid Mixed Earnings Results
feed.summary: The Honest Company (NASDAQ: HNST) saw its stock decline after reporting mixed fourth-quarter 2025 results, with earnings exceeding expectations but sales falling short. Meridian Contrarian Fund, managed by ArrowMark Partners, views the company's strategic decisions to exit the Canadian market and baby apparel business as positive steps towards focusing on profitable growth, despite its underperformance in the market. The fund, while reducing its position for tax losses, maintains a stake, believing the market undervalues Honest's long-term potential.

feed.title: 72M users and AI: inside Mercado Pago’s push to be LatAm’s biggest digital bank
feed.summary: Mercado Libre's fintech arm, Mercado Pago, aims to become the largest digital bank in Latin America, focusing on deep user engagement and "principality" for its 72 million active users. A recent podcast episode featuring Senior VP Andy Anavi highlighted the strategic evolution from a payments wallet to a full digital bank, emphasizing the transformative role of Generative AI in underwriting and personalized financial advisory to achieve this goal.
